opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I spilled boiling water from the kettle over my forearm about 45 minutes ago and the skin feels like it's still on fire. It's gone really red and it's so painful, especially when the air moves over it. What should I do?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It just happened and the pain is intense — I want to know if I need to go to hospital or if I can treat it at home.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 45 minutes ago, right after boiling water splashed on my arm
symptom_narrative.duration: since it happened, so about 45 minutes
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: pretty bad, maybe a 6 or 7 out of 10 — it's really sore
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the inside of my left forearm, a patch about the size of my palm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, and it stings more when air blows across it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: I held it under the cold tap for about 15 minutes and that helped a bit while it was on
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving my arm, and the air hitting the skin
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just the redness and soreness — no other problems
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had little kitchen burns before but nothing like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm holding my arm still and away from things because it hurts to move it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives on my own; it was an accident in the kitchen, the kettle cord was out of the way

objective_data_offered.value: a red patch about the size of my palm on the inside of my forearm; the skin looks dry and there are no blisters
objective_data_offered.timing: now, about 45 minutes after the spill

objective_data_offered.value: it looks a bit puffy and swollen around the red area
when I press on the red skin it goes pale and then the redness comes straight back
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-performed blanch test over chat — not a clinician-graded capillary refill assessment
